hero.headline: Amir Ibrahim | Computer Engineer
hero.subheadline: I build scalable systems that stay reliable when real-world pressure hits.
hero.bio: Computer Engineering grad from York University (2025). I worked professionally for 7+ years — managing a 13-person operations team and co-founding a live product — while earning my degree. I build software in Java, Python, and JavaScript, focused on reliability, clean architecture, and code that teams can actually extend.

projects.title: KonnectTaps — Digital Business Card Platform
projects.stack: Next.js, Python, MySQL, Ubuntu VPS, Nginx, PM2
projects.description: Deployed a full production stack on Ubuntu VPS (Nginx reverse proxy, Next.js frontend, Python backend, PM2, SSL via certbot). Built and iterated on the business card editor — element rendering, drag-and-drop positioning, per-element settings controls (100+ active users). Ran end-to-end QA and regression testing across production releases.

projects.title: MIX Registration System
projects.description: Built and iterated on the user-facing registration interface for an annual peak-traffic event. Implemented form flows, input validation states, and real-time feedback for the live registration window. Codebase is private.

projects.title: Automated Plant Watering System
projects.stack: Arduino, Java, MOSFET, Moisture Sensor, DC Water Pump
projects.description: Capacitive moisture sensor continuously monitors soil conditions. MOSFET-controlled DC water pump activates when moisture drops below threshold. Control logic written in Java via an Arduino-Java bridge library.

projects.title: Embedded Combination Lock
projects.stack: Arduino, Java, Potentiometer, Servo Motor, Push Buttons
projects.description: Potentiometer handles rotary code entry; push buttons confirm each input. Servo motor drives the physical lock mechanism based on validated input. State tracking, input validation, and hardware output control in Java.
